heading: Example
body:
The following index definition indexes the `title` field in the
[sample collection](custom-analyzers-eg-coll) collection using a custom
analyzer named `englishPossessiveStemmer`. The custom analyzer
specifies the following:

- Apply the [standard](standard-tokenizer-ref) tokenizer to
  create tokens (search terms) based on word break rules.

- Apply the [englishPossessive](englishPossessive-tf-ref)
  token filter to remove possessives (trailing `'s`) from the
  tokens.

Atlas UI

Visual Editor

- In the Custom Analyzers section, click Add
  Custom Analyzer.

- Select the Create Your Own radio button and click
  Next.

- Type whitespace in the Analyzer Name field.

- Expand Tokenizer if it's collapsed.

- Select whitespace from the dropdown.

- Expand Token Filters and click 
  Add token filter.

- Select wordDelimiterGraph from the dropdown.

- Click Add token filter to add the token filter to your
  custom analyzer.

- Click Add to create the custom analyzer.

- In the Field Mappings section, click Add
  Field Mapping to apply the custom analyzer on
  the message field.

- Select message from the Field
  Name dropdown and dateFacet from the Data
  Type dropdown.

- In the properties section for the data type, select whitespace
  from the Index Analyzer and Search Analyzer
  dropdowns.

- Click Add, then Save Changes.

JSON Editor

Replace the default index definition with the following example:

```json
{
  "mappings": {
    "fields": {
      "title": {
        "type": "string",
        "analyzer": "englishPossessiveStemmer"
      }
    }
  },
  "analyzers": [
    {
      "name": "englishPossessiveStemmer",
      "charFilters": [],
      "tokenizer": {
        "type": "standard"
      },
      "tokenFilters": [
        {
          "type": "englishPossessive"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

MongoDB Shell

The following query searches the `title` field in the
[sample collection](custom-analyzers-eg-coll) collection for the term
`team`.

Atlas UI

MongoDB Shell

MongoDB Search returns results that contain the term `team` in the
`title` field. MongoDB Search returns the document with `_id: 1` because
MongoDB Search transforms `team's` in the `title` field to the token
`team` during analysis. Specifically, MongoDB Search creates the following
tokens (searchable terms) for the documents in the results, which it
then matches to the query term:

| Document ID | Output Tokens                     |
| ----------- | --------------------------------- |
| "\_id": 1   | The, team, weekly, meeting        |
| "\_id": 2   | The, check, in, with, sales, team |
